qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Node panic on invalid blocks committed by 2/3+ validators enables consensus DoS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: the finding reports panics on invalid blocks with +2/3 votes in consensus/state.go, while the advisory concerns BFT Time derivation from commits whose signature verification did not enforce validator address/index consistency.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded transaction flood via RPC and P2P enables denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: the finding reports missing per-client or per-peer rate limiting in mempool transaction submission paths, not the advisory's commit signature verification and median block-time inconsistency.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe RPC endpoints lack authorization, enabling network manipulation and mempool attacks
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: the finding reports unauthenticated unsafe RPC methods gated by the unsafe configuration flag, not the advisory's consensus commit verification and BFT Time calculation flaw.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Signature cache uses signature-only keys, enabling cache poisoning attacks
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same broad signature-verification area, but different mechanism: the finding reports a signature-only cache key, not the advisory's validator address/index mismatch that lets block time be derived from a different validator mapping than signature verification used.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Vote extension signature verification can be bypassed, enabling consensus manipulation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: the finding reports inconsistent use of Verify() versus VerifyVoteAndExtension() for vote extensions, not the advisory's last-commit validator address/order inconsistency used by BFT Time.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: WebSocket endpoint accepts all cross-origin requests, enabling CSRF attacks
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: the finding reports an unconditional WebSocket CheckOrigin bypass in RPC handling, not the advisory's consensus commit-signature and BFT Time median-time inconsistency.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described BFT Time root cause. The advisory patch rejects commit signature validator address/index mismatches during commit verification and makes MedianTime error on unknown commit validator addresses; the reported findings cover invalid-block panics, mempool flooding, unsafe RPC methods, signature-cache keying, vote-extension verification, and WebSocket origin handling instead.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-c32p-wcqj-j677 describes inconsistencies between commit signature verification and block time derivation that break the BFT Time guarantee.
Reference patch bf8274fcdbcab2bc652660ae627196a90a6efb97 adds validator-address mismatch checks in types/validation.go verifyCommitBatch and verifyCommitSingle.
The same patch changes state.MedianTime and State.MakeBlock/validateBlock callers so unknown commit validator addresses return errors instead of being skipped during weighted median time calculation.

None of the final qualifying vulnerability reports describes the validator address/index mismatch, unknown-validator median-time behavior, or a source-to-sink path that lets commit signatures verify under one validator mapping while block time is derived under another.
